notes:
Game starts and shows the mouse cursor as well as the epilepsy warning screen, then crashes after a few seconds. Installed Uplay through Wine and started it via Steam/Proton. 

Tried all the tweaks: PROTON_NO_ESYNC doesn't make a difference. Using PROTON_USE_WINED3D, the game won't even start anymore ("Failed to enumerate DXGI adapter"). With PROTON_NO_D3D11 Uplay won't start.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Turned off the UPLAY overlay and the Steam overlay. Also used DXVK 1.0.3 which has Anno 1800 performance improvements. Game loads the first warning screen and you can see the mouse cursor as well, but a few seconds later the game crashes to the desktop.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Uplay launcher runs but game throws a C++ runtime error on start.
title: Anno 1800

notes:
Game launches and crashes to desktop a few seconds after reaching a black loading screen with epilepsy warning text and a yellow mouse cursor.

Tried the usual proton variables in the launch options (Disable Esync, D3d11, Use Wine D3d11)  with no luck.

I also tried disabling the Uplay overlay without success.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Disable Uplay ingame overlay & you must use uplay in offline mode, or it won't work at all. After those things singleplayer works perfectly!
title: Anno 1800

notes: With the UPlay Overlay disabled, and UPlay launched in offline mode, the game will run. It's certainly playable, though the missing Multiplayer is a bit of a bummer
title: Anno 1800

notes:
disabled ingame overlay + enabled Uplay offline mode + disabled Esync. 
Just starts into ANNO startscreen and then crashes with error popup of "Wine C++ Runtime Library": "Program: Z"
title: Anno 1800

notes: It works great now after I disabled the overlay from UPLAY and making UPLAY run in offline mode. Those are the needed tweaks to make this game work.
title: Anno 1800

notes:
Disable Uplay ingame overlay & you must use uplay in offline mode, or it won't work at all. After those things singleplayer works perfectly!
It took a few tries to get the game started. Now the singleplayer is running.
title: Anno 1800

notes: disabled uplay ingame overlay and set uplay to offline mode. after that the singleplayer runs just fine.
title: Anno 1800

notes: I've tried to disable overlay of steam and uplay and running uplay in offline. But still game mostly crashes on start. I managed to run it 3 times and game was running nicely, but I've no idea how. Anyway if you have issue with not opening uplay, try install vulkan drivers https://github.com/lutris/lutris/wiki/Installing-drivers worked for me.
title: Anno 1800

notes:
Downloaded game and installed with no issues but game crashed on launch. To fix I did the following:-

1. Select offline mode in Uplay. 
2. I have disabled Esync  in the Anno 1800 launch options in Steam. 

Game runs fairly smooth with the odd stutter now and then
title: Anno 1800

notes: Also get the error "Wine C++ Runtime Library": "Program: Z" launching with overlays disabled and uplay offline mode. The same error on lutris with wine 4.5 (ubuntu 18.10 issue? open beta was working fine on my system)
title: Anno 1800

notes: Following the instructions in previous reviews, I disabled the UPlay overlay, set it to offline mode and the campaign runs great.
title: Anno 1800

notes: I updated Ubuntu from 18.10 to 19.04 for newer mesa. No overlay + offline. Still running into "Wine C++ Runtime Library":
title: Anno 1800

notes: Game launches, but stays at a black screen with the ingame cursor. Putting UPlay in offline mode doesn't change anything in my case. The open beta worked fine for me with the same environment, even with UPlay in online mode.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Uplay launches, Game goes full screen. Borks after the epilepsy warning.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Getting error "Wine C++ Runtime Library", unable to play. However found workaround with lutris+tkg-protonified4.1. Run uplay, go to offline mode, start anno, kill Anno1800.exe process (first time). Click on play in uplay again and game should run without error. Work for me every time so far. Game is kinda laggy (not depending on quality settings) but playable.
title: Anno 1800

notes:
Out of the box, Anno 1800 will crash right after the epilepsy warning screen. However, you can make the single player part of the game work by disabling the Uplay overlay and switching Uplay into offline mode.

This can be done by editing the file ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/916440/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Local Settings/Application Data/Ubisoft Game Launcher/settings.yml and setting overlay.enabled = false and user.forceoffline = true. Next time you start Anno 1800 via Steam, the Uplay login window opens but won't let you log in because you are in offline mode. Click on "Settings" and *unset* the checkbox for "Always start Uplay in offline mode", then click on the small "Restart Uplay" button above it. Now you can log in and don't forget to set the "Remember Me" checkbox. After logging in successfully, you can now reenable offline mode (e.g. by closing uplay, and setting user.forceoffline = true in the config file again.

Multiplayer won't work that way, obviously. Also, zooming in/out causes a few graphical artifacts, but that might be solved by a newer DXVK version. Other than that, the game is perfectly playable.
title: Anno 1800

notes: On Fedora 30 with Flatpak Steam, Anno 1800 plays with Windows like performance, stable 60 FPS on High (GTX960). Only tweaks I made was to disable the Uplay overlay (Uplay -> Settings -> General -> "Enable in-game overlay for supported games"). To prevent the game from crashing when connecting to online services, start Uplay in offline mode (Uplay -> Settings -> Network -> "Always start Uplay in offline mode").
title: Anno 1800

notes: After todays patch (patch 1), the game runs without any noticable flaws. I did disable the UPlay overlay but did not need to explicitly start UPlay in offline mode (online still didn't work though).
title: Anno 1800

notes: Runs fine since last update, but random crashes do happen
title: Anno 1800

notes: Still not working even with Patch01 of Anno. Crashes at startscreen into error popup ""Wine C++ Runtime Library". UPlay offline + disabled overlay.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Wine C++ Error, even with Offline mode and no overlay
title: Anno 1800

notes: Single player works fine in Offline mode with Overlay disabled. Multiplayer doesn't.
title: Anno 1800

notes: I was able to workaround the c++ runtime error and can now start the game with uplay offline and overlays disabled(github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2544). Ingame rendering seems fine but the grass on the islands is green.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Runs perfectly once you disable the uplay overlay and run uplay in offline mode
title: Anno 1800

notes: Ran OK with uPlay in offline mode and overlay disabled. All graphics settings on maximum/Ultra-High. Regardless of graphics settings game has a bit of lag loading assets not present loaded from the same SSD on windows.
title: Anno 1800

title: Anno 1800

notes: Under 4.2-9, the game ran smoothly (with uplay in offline mode) 4.11-1 seriously glitches out the graphics. Most island geometry doesn't render, and the fog does not reveal as ships move through. Using Wine D3d11 renders all of the geometry, but doesn't apply textures. This coincides with game update 4 and Sunken Treasures DLC, which are the likely culprits of this seeming regression.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Started a new sandbox, with a mix of medium-high settings getting 75 FPS (which is my monitor cap)  at 3440x1440p; I have used the existing installer script from lutris without any tweaks.
title: Anno 1800

notes: Just works™. Game seems to crash at random times, but (mostly windows) users of the anno subreddit have reported a large number of game-breaking bugs and crashes, so I don't think it's proton specific.
title: Anno 1800
